nysed-ela-2024-g4-mc-q1.alt: Question 1. What does the word “banks” mean as it is used in paragraph 1? Choices: A: angles in a road B: rows of things that are like each other C: sloping pieces of land D: places where people work with money

nysed-ela-2024-g4-mc-q2.alt: Question 2. What does the word “task” mean as it is used in paragraph 3? Choices: A: a way of moving something heavy B: a job that needs to be done C: a plan for making something happen D: a reason to go to a new place

nysed-ela-2024-g4-mc-q3.alt: Question 3. Which sentence best explains what happens in the section “A Towering Tomb”? Choices: A: Workers drag stones up a ramp to place in a pyramid. B: The king decides that he wants the tallest pyramid. C: Workers come from different places to build a pyramid. D: The king realizes that he needs more space in the pyramid.

nysed-ela-2024-g4-mc-q4.alt: Question 4. How does the illustration help the reader understand the information in paragraph 6? Choices: A: It shows how the parts of the pyramid are connected. B: It shows why the pyramid needs to be so large. C: It shows that there is an underground part of the pyramid. D: It shows that there is an area in the pyramid for the queen.

nysed-ela-2024-g4-mc-q5.alt: Question 5. Which sentence from the article best supports the idea that people came from all over the country to work for the pharaoh? Choices: A: “Others were pulling huge stones up the slanted sides of a building.” (paragraph 2) B: “Building the Great Pyramid took lots of planning.” (paragraph 6) C: “No one knows how the workers got the stones to the top of the pyramid.” (paragraph 8) D: “Workers came to Giza from every part of Egypt to help build Khufu’s tomb.” (paragraph 9)

nysed-ela-2024-g4-mc-q6.alt: Question 6. What is a main idea of the article? Choices: A: People were scared when they came to the banks of the Nile River. B: Egyptians had the important job of building a tomb for the king. C: People thought the king was a sky god who came down to Earth. D: Egyptians spent three or four months each year working on the pyramid.

nysed-ela-2024-g4-mc-q19.alt: Question 19. The phrase “play it cool” in paragraph 10 suggests that Abigail Iris Choices: A: does not want to make any noise B: does not want to feel foolish C: does not want to seem too eager D: does not want to look impolite

nysed-ela-2024-g4-mc-q20.alt: Question 20. Which detail would be most important to include in a summary of the story? Choices: A: “They are already above the popcorn I always get when we’re done shopping, and they would move right up to number one...” (paragraph 2) B: “The cat lady puts down the book she’s reading and reaches into a cage and hands my mother the cat I picked for her.” (paragraph 5) C: “All I see in there is a big stuffed teddy bear, and I’m thinking the cat- rescue lady might need to wear better glasses when she goes out...” (paragraph 6) D: “He crawls up on my chest and buries his nose with the orange spot on it into my neck, and then he’s very still...” (paragraph 13)

nysed-ela-2024-g4-mc-q21.alt: Question 21. Which detail from the story shows how Abigail Iris’s mother feels about her daughter’s request to have a pet? Choices: A: “‘Well, I don’t know,’ she says, smiling. ‘You have to be very gentle.’” (paragraph 12) B: “‘What a baby,’ she says about my kitten.” (paragraph 15) C: “‘He’s very sweet,’ my mother says.” (paragraph 17) D: “See what he thinks about a cat now that you all are older. Maybe it’s time.” (paragraph 19)

nysed-ela-2024-g4-mc-q22.alt: Question 22. What does the lady at the cat rescue most likely believe about Abigail Iris? Choices: A: Abigail Iris is too young to care for a cat. B: Abigail Iris would be a good owner for Spot. C: Abigail Iris does not understand cats’ needs. D: Abigail Iris should get the older gray cat.

nysed-ela-2024-g4-mc-q23.alt: Question 23. Some people believe that pets can encourage responsibility. How do Abigail Iris’s actions in the story support this belief? Choices: A: Abigail Iris and Mom think that a pet should be loved by a whole family so Mom asks Dad what he thinks. B: Abigail Iris spends more time with her mom by going to the market as part of her plan to get a cat. C: Abigail Iris clearly finds Spot to be cute and charming even though her parents do not want a cat. D: Abigail Iris says she is willing to do new chores and work hard because she loves Spot immediately.

nysed-ela-2024-g4-mc-q26.alt: Question 26. What is a central idea of paragraphs 2 through 4? Choices: A: Changes in climate are affecting the production of chocolate. B: Chocolate comes from a narrow area near the equator. C: Some cacao trees grow where tropical rainforests are located. D: The chocolate being made today is of lower quality than in the past.

nysed-ela-2024-g4-mc-q27.alt: Question 27. According to paragraphs 7 and 8, how are weather stations helping to save chocolate? Choices: A: They help trees survive during times of little rain. B: They provide farmers advice on treating tree diseases. C: They give information about planting and caring for trees. D: They make rules for farmers to follow for each season.

nysed-ela-2024-g4-mc-q28.alt: Question 28. What does the phrase “a sweet deal” mean as used in paragraph 9? Choices: A: dangerous sign B: new beginning C: difficult challenge D: good result

nysed-ela-2024-g4-mc-q29.alt: Question 29. How is the section “Making Candy” organized? Choices: A: The author gives a detailed explanation about the similarities between cacao and cocoa. B: The author describes the steps used to make chocolate from growing cacao pods to preparing a chocolate bar for sale. C: The author provides detailed answers to frequently asked questions about how chocolate is grown and harvested. D: The author shows how chocolate makers are using new processes to improve the flavor of their product.

nysed-ela-2024-g4-mc-q30.alt: Question 30. How does the photograph after paragraph 14 best support an idea developed in the article? Choices: A: It explains why machines are not useful for harvesting pods. B: It shows how the seeds or beans are separated from the pods. C: It gives an example of how climate change has damaged the pods. D: It shows why scientists want to develop trees that produce more pods.

nysed-ela-2024-g4-mc-q31.alt: Question 31. Many people enjoy eating chocolate. Which quote best supports this idea? Choices: A: “Last year, Americans spent more than $22 billion on chocolate.” (paragraph 1) B: “You need more of it to make chocolate taste, well, chocolatey.” (paragraph 3) C: “Then the liquid chocolate is poured into molds and prepared for sale.” (paragraph 17) D: “This is the moment you’ve been waiting for.” (paragraph 18)
